title: Government Intensifying Efforts to Boost Horticulture Production, Says Agriculture State Minister
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- The Government of Ethiopia has been intensifying its effort to boost horticulture, design policies and strategies that attract both local and foreign investors into the sector, Agriculture State Minister Sofia Kassa said.

title: Local Investors Take Lead in Ethiopia's SEZs As Reforms Pay Off
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Domestic investors now account for 58.5% of active investments in Ethiopia's Special Economic Zones (SEZs), signaling a major shift driven by recent policy reforms and targeted incentives, according to the Industrial Parks Development Corporation (IPDC).

title: Innovation Reshaping Financial Landscape of Ethiopia - National Bank Governor
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Innovation is reshaping the financial landscape in Ethiopia as the country continues its financial reform and economic transformation agenda, National Bank of Ethiopia Governor Eyob Tekalign said.

title: Ethiopia's Corridor Dev't Initiatives Delivering Far-Reaching Socio-Economic Benefits, Says PM Abiy
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed has underscored that Ethiopia's ongoing corridor development initiatives are delivering far-reaching economic and social benefits, extending well beyond urban beautification.

title: Ethiopian Airlines to Lease Two Next-Generation Converted Freighters From Aercap
summary: [Reporter] Ethiopian Airlines has agreed to lease two Boeing 777-300ER converted freighters from AerCap Holdings N.V., in a deal that will introduce the aircraft type to Africa for the first time, the companies said on Tuesday.

title: Ethiopia Somali Region President Registers As Voters Surge
summary: [Horn Diplomat] Dhagax-Buur, Ethiopia, March 24 (Horn Diplomat) -- President of Ethiopia's Somali Regional State, Mustafe Mohamed Omar, accompanied by members of the ruling Prosperity Party, registered to vote on Monday in Degahbour town in Jarar zone, as voter registration continues ahead of the country's upcoming general elections.

summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- The Ministry of Agriculture announced that Ethiopia has launched an ambitious national program to expand sorghum production, aiming to strengthen food self-sufficiency, reduce import dependence and support agro-industrial growth.

title: Deputy PM Highlights Health Sector Transformation in Amhara Region of Ethiopia
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Deputy Prime Minister Temesgen Tiruneh has praised the ongoing transformation at the Amhara Regional Health Bureau, describing it as a reform effort driven not only by systems but by a clear and urgent purpose.

title: Ethiopia Pushes Agribusiness Overhaul As Minister Calls for Stronger Private Investment to Secure Food Sovereignty
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopia is stepping up efforts to transform its agribusiness sector, with senior officials calling for stronger private investment to achieve food sovereignty and reduce reliance on imports.

title: Ballot Draw Ceremony Underway for Ethiopia's Seventh General Election
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- The draw ceremony to determine the order of names and symbols of political parties and independent candidates registered with the National Election Board of Ethiopia on the ballot paper is currently underway.

title: Ethiopia Remains a Beacon of Pan-Africanism - Zimbabwe's Chief Justice
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Zimbabwe's Chief Justice, Luke Malaba underscored Ethiopia's firm commitment to freedom and human dignity has solidified its position as a premier symbol of Pan-Africanism.

title: Ethiopia, India Forge Stronger AI Alliance With High-Level Tech Visit
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopia and India have taken a significant step toward strengthening bilateral cooperation in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and digital innovation.

title: La IFRC advierte: la crisis de Sudán se extiende más allá de las fronteras a medida que se agrava la crisis humanitaria
summary: Juba, Nairobi, Ginebra, 17 de marzo de 2026 – Los efectos del conflicto en curso en Sudán se dejan sentir mucho más allá de sus fronteras. La Federación Internacional de Sociedades de la Cruz Roja y de la Media Luna Roja (IFRC) advierte que la situación humanitaria regional en Chad, Egipto, Etiopía, Sudán del Sur y Uganda se está deteriorando rápidamente, a medida que se agotan los fondos y las necesidades siguen aumentando. Casi 4,5 millones de personas han huido de Sudán hacia los países vecin
source_name: International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies

title: Migration along the Eastern route - Report 62 | January - December 2025, Publication: 23 March 2026
summary: About the Report The Eastern Route is one of the busiest and riskiest migration routes in the world travelled by hundreds of thousands of migrants, most of whom travel irregularly, often relying on smugglers to facilitate movement. This report provides updates on complex migratory dynamics through Ethiopia, Djibouti, Somalia and Yemen based on diverse data sources and consultations with key informants. title: Education in Danger Monthly Brief, February 2026
summary: Since the start of Insecurity Insight’s Education in Danger open-source monitoring in January 2017 and February 2026, on average 63 incidents per month were identified, covering both political and conflict violence, averaging 2.1 attacks on education every day. The average rose from 32 per month in 2017-2022, to 168 per month in 2025, reflecting both improved open-source reporting as well as a growing frequency in which education comes under attack. summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed has officially inaugurated the newly developed Niin Lee Palm Lodge in Afar region, marking a major milestone under the government's 'Dine for Generations' initiative.

summary: [Daba Finance] Ethiopian Securities Exchange has launched Neway, its first web and mobile trading platform, as the country builds out infrastructure for its capital market.

title: Locust Watch: Sub/seasonal precipitation predictions in the Desert Locust spring and summer breeding areas (April - September 2026) (19 March 2026)
summary: Climate models continue to indicate a rapid transition to El Niño in the Pacific by late spring, combined with a positive Indian Ocean Dipole. These signals are expected to generate wetter-than-normal conditions across all Regions during the spring. In summer, wet conditions may persist in the Western Region, while drier-than-normal conditions are forecast for Eastern African, the Arabian Peninsula and the Eastern Region. Spring breeding is expected to remain small‑scale in Saudi Arabia, Yemen,
source_name: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations

title: How the World's Muslims Celebrate Eid 2026?
summary: [ENA] As the sun rose over a Middle East scarred by weeks of relentless conflict, the call to prayer resonated amidst the distant thunder of the front lines. Yet the region did not retreat into silence. Instead, it responded with a defiant, multi-colored symphony of tradition. Since the opening salvos weeks ago, a war many feared. But few truly expected with such speed, has reshaped the emotional and economic landscape of the region.

title: AU Commission Chairperson Extends Eid Al-Fitr Greetings, Calls for Unity and Peace
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- On the occasion of Eid al-Fitr, the Chairperson of the African Union Commission, Mouhamed Ali Yousuf, has extended warm greetings to Muslims across Africa and the world.

title: Ethiopia's Urban Safety Net Program Empowers Citizens Toward Economic Independence, Ministry Reports
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Urban Development Safety Net Program is helping citizens move from dependency to economic productivity, the Ministry of Urban and Infrastructure announced.

title: Milking Opportunity - One Farmer's Journey Reflects the Job Potential of Ethiopia's Dairy Sector
summary: [World Bank] In rural Ethiopia, smallholder farmer Ayelech Tarekegn Mekebo transformed a single local cow into a productive dairy enterprise with support from the World Bank-financed Livestock and Fisheries Sector Development Project. Through improved breeding, cooperative membership, and union-level processing, she now earns steady income and dividends. Her journey shows how targeted investments unlock rural jobs and empower women farmers.

title: Ethiopian Muslims Celebrate 1447th Eid Al-Fitr in Unity and Joy
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopian Muslims across the country are celebrating the 1447th Eid al-Fitr with deep spiritual devotion, vibrant gatherings, and a renewed sense of unity, marking the end of the holy month of Ramadan.

title: President Taye Extends Eid Al-Fitr Greetings to Muslims
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- President Taye Atske Selassie has extended his heartfelt greetings to Muslims celebrating Eid al-Fitr, marking the end of the holy month of Ramadan.

title: Oil Price Surge Is Hurting African Economies - Scholars in Ethiopia, Kenya, Nigeria, Senegal and South Africa Take Stock
summary: [The Conversation Africa] The attacks by the US and Israel on Iran, which started on 28 February 2026, upended key supply chains, driving oil prices above US$100 a barrel. The spike followed Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response to the US and Israeli action. About 20% of the world's oil supplies are transported through the strait.

summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Finance Minister, Ahmed Shide held a meeting with Chinese Ambassador to Ethiopia, Chen Hai, and a delegation from the Export-Import Bank of China (China Exim Bank) to discuss matters of mutual economic interest.

title: 100,000 Flee South Sudan Into Ethiopia, According to Unicef, Amid Violence
summary: [RFI] South Sudan's army forced an estimated 100,000 people to flee across the border into Ethiopia after ordering the evacuation of the opposition-held town of Akobo earlier this month, Unicef said Tuesday.

summary: Drought hotspots across Eastern Africa, including eastern Kenya, Somalia, central Uganda, southern Ethiopia, and parts of Tanzania, are recovering following February 2026 rains. summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Italy has reaffirmed its commitment to support Ethiopia's flagship infrastructure projects, pledging financing for the Koysha Hydropower Project and key components of the planned Bishoftu Airport, as both nations deepen strategic economic cooperation.
